claim: Model performance is becoming a secondary constraint; the real limitation is now the agent architecture and the agentic ecosystem you build around the models.
actionable: Shift evaluation focus from benchmark scores to agent workflows; invest in agent architecture (skills, orchestration, sandboxes) rather than chasing the latest model.
source_title: I gave Gemini 3 Pro its own computer

claim: Agents can execute isolated computing tasks in sandboxes; spinning up multiple agent instances in parallel on the same task enables 'best-of-n' selection.
actionable: Deploy 5-15 agent instances in parallel sandboxes for critical tasks; evaluate the best output and discard failures rather than optimizing for single-agent perfection.
source_title: I gave Gemini 3 Pro its own computer

claim: Multi-turn, full-stack application building (not simple UIs) is the real benchmark for agentic capability; agents can build Python backends, frontend, and databases in one shot.
actionable: Test your agents on multi-turn full-stack tasks (CRUD apps, multi-feature systems); simple UI generation is trivial; focus on end-to-end system building.
source_title: I gave Gemini 3 Pro its own computer

claim: Agent orchestration via memory files and skill systems allows reprogramming agent behavior (e.g., backslash commands); this is more impactful than model selection.
actionable: Build memory files and skill systems that teach agents custom syntax (e.g., backslash commands); use these to orchestrate workflows without changing the underlying model.
source_title: I gave Gemini 3 Pro its own computer

claim: Tool calling benchmarks (terminal bench, agentic work duration) matter more than pure reasoning benchmarks; models that excel at long-duration tool-using tasks outperform high-reasoning models.
actionable: When selecting models for agents, prioritize tool-calling and long-duration task duration over reasoning benchmarks; test on realistic multi-step workflows.
source_title: I gave Gemini 3 Pro its own computer

claim: Agents are only using 50-60% of a model's intelligence potential; the gap between current agent utilization and maximum capability is massive.
actionable: Assume you're leaving 40-50% of model performance on the table; invest in agent architecture improvements (prompting, orchestration, tools) before upgrading models.
source_title: I gave Gemini 3 Pro its own computer

claim: Context window compression and tool-calling deletion in newer models (e.g., Codex) may improve efficiency but should be verified before adoption.
actionable: When upgrading to new models, test for context window behavior changes; compression techniques can improve efficiency but may require workflow adjustments.
source_title: I gave Gemini 3 Pro its own computer
